title: Unimate - First Industrial Robot
description: General Motors installed Unimate, the first industrial robot, to perform spot welding on an assembly line. This marked the beginning of robotic automation in manufacturing.

title: Shakey - First Mobile Robot with AI
description: SRI International developed Shakey, the first mobile robot to reason about its actions. It combined computer vision, natural language processing, and planning.

title: Honda E0 - Humanoid Research Begins
description: Honda secretly began developing humanoid robots with the E0 prototype, launching a multi-decade program that would eventually produce ASIMO.

title: Deep Blue Defeats Kasparov
description: While not a physical robot, Deep Blue's victory showed AI's growing capabilities, foreshadowing integration of advanced AI into robotic systems.

title: ASIMO Unveiled
description: Honda revealed ASIMO, a humanoid robot capable of walking, climbing stairs, and interacting with humans. It became an icon of humanoid robotics.

title: DARPA Grand Challenge
description: The first autonomous vehicle competition sparked innovation in mobile robotics, computer vision, and path planning algorithms.

title: ROS (Robot Operating System) Released
description: Willow Garage released ROS, providing a standardized framework for robot software development that would become industry standard.

title: Boston Dynamics Atlas
description: Boston Dynamics introduced Atlas, a hydraulically-powered humanoid robot designed for search and rescue. Later versions would perform backflips.

title: SoftBank Pepper Robot
description: The first emotion-reading humanoid robot designed for customer service and companionship, sold to consumers and businesses.

title: AlphaGo Defeats Lee Sedol
description: DeepMind's AlphaGo demonstrated superhuman performance in Go using deep reinforcement learning, a technique now used in robot control.

title: Boston Dynamics SpotMini
description: A four-legged robot demonstrating advanced mobility and manipulation, later commercialized as Spot for industrial inspection.

title: OpenAI Dactyl Robot Hand
description: OpenAI trained a robot hand to manipulate a Rubik's cube using reinforcement learning and sim-to-real transfer, showing the power of AI for dexterous manipulation.

title: Tesla AI Day - Dojo Supercomputer
description: Tesla announced Dojo, a supercomputer designed for training vision-based autonomous driving systems, advancing end-to-end learning for robots.

title: Tesla Optimus Announced
description: Elon Musk unveiled plans for Tesla Bot (Optimus), a general-purpose humanoid robot leveraging Tesla's AI and manufacturing capabilities.

title: NVIDIA Isaac Sim Released
description: NVIDIA launched Isaac Sim on Omniverse, providing photorealistic robot simulation for training AI models before deployment.

title: RT-2 - Vision-Language-Action Model
description: Google DeepMind introduced RT-2, combining vision-language models with robot control for generalizable manipulation tasks.

title: Figure AI Founded
description: Figure AI launched with $70M funding to develop commercially viable humanoid robots for labor automation, targeting manufacturing and logistics.

title: Figure 01 First Demo
description: Figure AI demonstrated Figure 01 performing autonomous tasks in a warehouse environment, showcasing end-to-end neural network control.

title: Unitree G1 Humanoid ($16K)
description: Chinese robotics company Unitree released the G1 humanoid robot at an unprecedented $16,000 price point, making humanoid robotics more accessible.

title: OpenAI Invests in Physical AI
description: OpenAI announced strategic investments in Figure AI and other robotics companies, signaling convergence of large language models and physical AI.

title: VLA Models Go Mainstream
description: Vision-Language-Action models become standard for robot control, enabling natural language commands and zero-shot generalization to new tasks.
